summary: Add the Keyword Expansion Agent with typed schemas, prompt construction, safe JSON completion wiring, and deterministic mock fallback.
acceptance_criteria: A reusable Keyword Expansion Agent module exists under agents/ with typed input, candidate output, Zod schemas, and a stable public function for generating keyword candidates.
The agent builds a prompt aligned with docs/08_ai_agents_and_prompts.md and calls the existing safeJsonCompletion() utility through an injected LlmClient when one is provided.
The agent provides a deterministic mock fallback that can generate 20 usable candidates without external API keys, process.env reads, network calls, database writes, or paid services.
Generated candidates are normalized, limited to the requested count, include country/language/intent/tool/rationale fields, and exclude configured excludedTopics with a case-insensitive guard.
Focused automated tests cover LLM success, malformed or rejected LLM output falling back to mock candidates, excluded topic filtering, deterministic fallback output, and requested count limits.
README documents the Keyword Expansion Agent slice, mock fallback behavior, no-secret/no-network policy, and the test command.

No SERP analysis, opportunity analysis, scoring engine, scan orchestration, database persistence, API route, UI, auth, cron, deployment, real LLM provider wiring, or paid service setup is implemented.
notes: Derived from docs/08_ai_agents_and_prompts.md Agent 1 prompt/schema and docs/11_development_plan.md recommended step 7 after LLM JSON client.

docs/09_codex_implementation_tasks.md mentions saving candidates to the database for Task 7; this smaller slice deliberately defers persistence to the later scan orchestrator/API task to keep the agent foundation verifiable and no-side-effect.
systems_architect is required because this introduces the agents/ boundary and will feed the scan pipeline.
Keep prompts centralized in agents/ and keep components/API routes out of scope.
non_goals: No SERP Analysis Agent, Opportunity Analysis Agent, scoring engine, MVP Spec Agent, or scan orchestrator.
No KeywordCandidate database writes or Prisma/lib changes.
No /api/scans/run route, dashboard integration, or UI changes.
No real LLM provider configuration, environment-variable secret loading, real network calls in tests, or paid service setup.
No changes to services/llm or services/serp contracts.
